steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The data shows that full-price restaurant customers leave worse comment ratios than discounted customers. The question asks which principle best explains why higher price (no discount) leads to more negative comments.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — introduces unrelated information: Brings in a factor not supported by the passage or data. ✗
  • Choice B — higher price raises expectations, making customers harder to satisfy: Directly explains the pattern: paying full price sets a higher bar, so the same experience is more likely to disappoint, producing worse comment ratios. ✓
  • Choice C — restates the observation: Only describes what the data already shows without explaining why. ✗
  • Choice D — suggests an unjustified pricing policy: Goes beyond the data and does not explain the comment-ratio gap. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
